questions.question: What's the actual impact of fees and slippage on retail-size paper-traded crypto strategies?
questions.summary: ~$1/round-trip on $200 positions (0.25% taker both legs). Gross +$1,085 became net -$782 on the 3-month backtest — the delta is the answer. Fees erase thin edges.

questions.question: Which parameter ranges of Bollinger and MA-crossover are robust to regime shifts, and which are overfit?
questions.summary: None are robust: all 12 variants with n>100 negative over 6 months net of costs. Per-regime conditioning untested (rung 3).

questions.question: (Emergent) Do constraints or strategies determine outcomes at this scale?
questions.summary: Constraints dominate: 99.6% of candidate signals died on cooldown/exposure/concurrency. Candidate fitness = edge per constraint slot, not signal frequency.

questions.question: Does sentiment data (Fear & Greed) improve technical strategies, or is it noise?
questions.summary: Layer 2 (context) never went live; deferred past Phase 1b per one-factor-at-a-time.

questions.question: Does the walk-forward tuner produce variants that hold up out-of-sample?
questions.summary: tune.py never built; compare.py (the scoring instrument) now exists. Blocked on the candidate roster accumulating live trades.

learnings.claim: GitHub Actions free-tier */5 cron delivers only ~3-5% of scheduled invocations for 5-minute schedules on small/inactive public repos, not the documented 70-85%.
learnings.evidence: 2026-05-23 migration entry (69 of 1,712 = 4.0% invocation rate over 6 days) and 2026-05-18 entry (~60min actual cadence vs 5min documented)

learnings.claim: Migrating the cron to a Hetzner CX22 VPS makes the 95% uptime gate achievable as written without amending Phase 2 entry criteria.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 47-day-gap entry: 11,574 runs all status='ok', zero gaps >1h; the 95%-uptime architectural gate is mechanically satisfied

learnings.claim: The infrastructure was the project — a monitoring system running unattended accumulates uptime evidence but produces zero trades/signals/decisions and no curriculum progress.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 47-day-gap entry: signals/trades/decisions/llm_calls all still 0 over 47 days while runs went 84 -> 11,574

learnings.claim: Elapsed calendar time did not produce learning: nearly everything the project learned came in the ~48 hours of 2026-07-16/17, and eleven weeks of calendar bought only one day of live trade-days.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 'Correction: the sequence was valuable; the duration was not' entry; live clock started 2026-07-16

learnings.claim: The fee floor kills gross-positive strategies: the ~0.6% round-trip cost exceeds the raw edge, so a strategy can be gross-positive yet net-negative.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 gauntlet entry: weekend_illiquidity_momentum gross-positive (win rate 49.4%, gross Sharpe 1.51) but fee-negative; all five candidates lost net of fees

learnings.claim: Both base textbook strategies (Bollinger and MA-crossover) fail to clear the bar over a real window; every variant with n>100 is net-negative.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 retirement entry: 6-month replay, net -$1,719.44 (-0.13%); bollinger_default n=539 at -$636.75; only positives are noise at n=3 and n=13

learnings.claim: Maker (limit) fills beat taker fills mechanically, saving ~$0.21-0.24 fees/trade with near-zero fill loss for drawdown-buying strategies because price falls into the resting limit.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 'First net-positive cell' entry: maker beat taker in 12/12 matched pairs, mean +$0.14/slot, fill loss 0-4 trades per ~150

learnings.claim: The +0.3% kill bar has a 21% false-positive rate at n=7, quantitatively vindicating the n<30 quarantine rule.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 power-calibration entry; reports/power-calibration-2026-07-18.json

learnings.claim: Detection power is near zero where interesting edges live: a true +0.17%-net edge passes only 32% at n=100 and less with more data; reliable detection starts ~+0.6% net.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 power-calibration entry (bootstrap of null arm's 930d returns, n=7,068)

learnings.claim: The meta-conclusion '5-min OHLCV has no edge' is only defensible as 'no edge >= ~0.6% net detectable by this instrument'; smaller edges remain open.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 power-calibration entry, decision (c) downgrading the meta-conclusion

learnings.claim: Sub-bar real edges are unrecoverable one-at-a-time because the fee floor binds per deployed rule, not per signal — the strongest argument for a combination/netting stage.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 power-calibration finding 3; reinforced by 2026-07-18 critique-driven reorder entry (netting, not diversification)

learnings.claim: Fresh, unanchored LLM context produces more validated learning than accumulated pipeline context because the pipeline's own context accumulates anchoring doctrine while a fresh context re-derives from evidence alone.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 'Outside eyes institutionalized' entry: ceiling study, power calibration, netting insight, slow-band prior, cost decomposition all trace to the blind-elicitation exercise

learnings.claim: The claude.ai Gmail connector is draft-only and cannot send email; Hetzner blocks outbound port 465 but leaves 587 open, so digest delivery requires SMTP via 587/STARTTLS.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 'Seam closed for real' entry: delivery confirmed via VPS scripts/send_digest.py on 587

learnings.claim: Growing synthesis LENSES from 5 to 8 silently exceeded max_tokens, truncating tool input that validated as {} and died with a misleading 'field missing' error.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 'Seam closed for real' bug-found section; fixed via lens rotation, max_tokens 16384, and loud raise on stop_reason=max_tokens

learnings.claim: First-venue measurements show the cost floor is per-symbol and partly a choice: Alpaca spreads 0.07% (BTC) to 0.42% (SOL) vs Binance <=0.015%; only slippage requires real trades to measure.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 critique-driven reorder entry, item 4

learnings.claim: The fixed-n promotion gate (p<0.05 at 100+ closes) has measured detection power of only ~20-25%, so it is replaced by a group-sequential design.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-18 'Four rulings from the learning-maximization review' entry, ruling 3

learnings.claim: A full git-history search confirms no real credential was ever committed to the repo; the May 2026 .env exposure surface was the Claude Code session transcript, not the repository.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 post-incident record entry (git-history search 2026-07-16)

learnings.claim: Logging incidents late destroys the facts: the May 2026 .env exposure record written ~60 days later from memory lost the exact date, the echoing command, and whether the transcript persisted.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 post-incident record, lesson 1

learnings.claim: The shared position ceiling means adding variants does not add exposure — they compete for the same $1,000 cap — so all 12 variants could run in parallel on Day 1 with no risk cost.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 'Front-loaded all 12 variants' entry: 99.6% of candidate signals rejected, total exposure never breached

learnings.claim: Constraints dominate strategy: cooldown/exposure/concurrency rejected 99.6% of candidate signals in the 6-month replay.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 retirement entry (1,371 trades / 390,519 candidates) and 2026-07-16 null-variant entry

learnings.claim: The rejection_streak_gated_ignition (r002) gate family is positive on the trailing 180d under both fill models but died over 930d, so the recency signal is unconfirmed (n=35, no claim).
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-17 'Seam closed for real' watchlist decision; scheduled out-of-sample re-test on the next 180d window

hypotheses.commitment: Program-level kill criterion v2: run the first scheduled ceiling study under a locked analysis plan, computing net_edge(h) = gross_edge(h) - turnover(h) x RTC; futility trigger fires if the one-sided 90% upper CB of net_edge is below zero at every horizon h in {24h,3d,1w,2w}.

Execution-cost experiment (pre-registration E-1.0): measure the true round-trip cost distribution via ~50 tiny live Alpaca trades under a locked protocol; no order fires until Alpaca live account is approved+funded and keys delivered.

By end of Week 2, commit a decision-log entry choosing one of three scheduler options (keep GH cron for fetch+signals move execute; move signals+execute together; or move everything); if none committed by end of Week 2, the Week 3 build does not begin.
hypotheses.resolution: Resolved by the 2026-05-23 migration entry, which moved the cron to a Hetzner VPS ahead of the Week 2 deadline.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of the first calendar week of cron operation, the runs table should contain ~2,016 rows with status='ok' (90%+ success rate); if below 90%, investigate before Week 2 strategy-roster review begins.
hypotheses.resolution: Falsified by 2026-05-18/2026-05-23 findings: GitHub Actions delivered only ~4% invocation rate, far below 90%; triggered the recalibration and VPS migration.

hypotheses.commitment: Amended from the 2026-05-17 hypothesis: track invocation rate (VPS-controlled, target >=99%) and code success rate (project-controlled, target >=95%) separately, with combined target 95%+ uptime of expected runs over any 4-week window.
hypotheses.resolution: Satisfied per 2026-07-02 entry: 11,574 runs all status='ok', 95% uptime architectural gate mechanically satisfied.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of Week 2, the Claude-mediated synthesis will produce either a defensible justification for keeping Bollinger and MA-crossover (naming modern-filter criteria passed/failed) or a defensible alternative roster; if neither achievable in two weeks, Week 3 starts a postmortem on the reframe itself.
hypotheses.resolution: Resolved by 2026-07-02 retirement entry: the 6-month replay produced a clean defensible negative and both base strategies were retired in favor of LLM-surfaced candidates.

hypotheses.commitment: If neither base strategy clears the bar over a real window, replace them.
hypotheses.resolution: Resolved by 2026-07-02 retirement entry: 6-month replay showed every n>100 variant net-negative; both strategies retired.

hypotheses.commitment: Original 2026-05-17 hypothesis: 90%+ success rate by end of the first calendar week of GitHub-Actions-invoked runs.
hypotheses.resolution: Amended and effectively falsified by the 2026-05-23 migration entry, which noted the platform-induced ceiling made the original hypothesis unfalsifiable and split it into invocation vs code-success metrics.
